text:
This entry focuses on the specialized terminology as it is used by Muhammad Said Ramadan al-Buti in his book al-Salafiyyah. It deals with the so-called ‘Arabian scales’, the ‘scientific and ideological shield’, and the ‘compass’ that the Sheikh identifies as the fundamental components of the manhaj of the righteous predecessors (salaf). In other words, these concepts are the components that form the foundation of the methodology of the salaf based on both the principle of faith and the eloquence of the original language of the Arabic speech.

By synthesizing the evidence from throughout the book, this research delves into the identity of the mainstream Muslim scholars that have conformed to the traditional “scales of sanity” as well as those who have deviated from them and the resulting consequences on their theology. It also examines al-Buti’s stance towards the permissible limits of inquiry by arguing that he refrained from the extreme silence on investigatory questions upheld by the most conservative muta‘akhkhirun, while also objecting to the unbridled personal judgment that the most progressive isti’dadi reformists permitted themselves as authoritatively interpretable sources of the faith. Through the classification of knowledge and al-Buti’s distinctive use of language, the author shows how his method manages to achieve a moderation between these two limits and, thereby, to establish a new standard for what can be called Islamic theology in the modern era.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
